title: Timeout Wrapper for Promises
description: Wraps an async operation to enforce a maximum execution time.
explanation: The utility uses Promise.race to compete between the original promise and a timeout promise. This pattern is simple and works with any promise-based operation. It’s useful for preventing long-running operations from hanging indefinitely, improving robustness in network requests or heavy computations.

title: Password Strength Validator
description: Validates if a password meets minimum strength requirements with configurable rules.
explanation: This function checks passwords against common security requirements using regex for efficient pattern matching. It is useful for enforcing password policies in registration or settings forms to enhance security.

title: Recursively Rename Object Keys Using a Mapping Function
description: Recursively transforms all object keys in a nested structure using a provided mapping function.
explanation: The function recursively traverses objects and arrays, applying a key mapping function to every key. This is useful for normalizing API responses, converting between naming conventions, or standardizing internal data formats. The included snakeToCamel and camelToSnake utilities handle common transformations.

title: Format Duration into Human-Readable String
description: Converts a duration in milliseconds into a human-readable string like '2h 30m 15s'.
explanation: The utility breaks down milliseconds into days, hours, minutes, and seconds using integer division and modulus. It is useful for logging, progress tracking, or displaying elapsed time in UIs without requiring external libraries.

title: Mask Sensitive Strings
description: Masks parts of strings such as emails or credit cards by revealing only the first and last few characters.
explanation: The core utility splits strings into visible and masked segments, ensuring safety and readability. It handles edge cases like empty strings or overly long masks gracefully, making it reusable across various sensitive data scenarios.

title: Deeply Flatten Nested Arrays
description: Recursively flattens an array of arbitrarily nested arrays into a single-level array.
explanation: Uses Array.reduce with recursion to handle arbitrary nesting depths. Providing shallow and depth-limited variants ensures performance control for common cases while handling deep structures when needed.

title: Toggle Dark Mode Utility
description: Toggle between dark and light mode by manipulating the document's theme and storing preference in localStorage.
explanation: Uses a CSS class 'dark-mode' to switch themes, persists preference via localStorage, and respects system preference on first load. This keeps UI consistent across sessions while allowing user override.

title: Min-Heap Priority Queue
description: Implements a generic min-heap priority queue with efficient insert and extract-min operations.
explanation: This min-heap implementation uses an array-based binary heap structure to efficiently manage priorities. Insertion and extraction both operate in O(log n) time due to the heapify-up and heapify-down operations. It's useful for scheduling tasks, Dijkstra's algorithm, or any scenario where you need to process items by priority.

title: Once Function
description: Creates a function that invokes the given function only once and caches the result.
explanation: The once function ensures a given function runs only once, which is useful for initialization tasks or expensive operations. The memoize function caches results based on input arguments, improving performance for repeated calls with the same arguments. Throttle limits how often a function can be called, which is helpful for event handlers.

title: Format and Parse Numbers with Locale Support
description: Formats a number with thousands separators and optional decimal places using browser locale, or parses a formatted string back to a number.
explanation: Uses Intl.NumberFormat for locale-aware formatting and parsing, ensuring correct separators for any locale without external dependencies. The approach handles both formatting and reverse parsing robustly by inspecting the locale's decimal separator dynamically.

title: Exponential Backoff Retry Utility
description: Executes an async function with automatic retries using exponential backoff.
explanation: The utility implements exponential backoff with jitter to prevent thundering herd problems during retries. It's useful for network requests or operations that may temporarily fail due to transient issues.

title: Strong Password Validator
description: Checks if a password meets common security requirements.
explanation: The function uses regex to efficiently check for required character types and length. It's useful because it provides flexible validation that can adapt to different security policies without external dependencies.

title: Deep Flatten Object
description: Recursively flattens a nested object into a single-level object with dot-notation keys.
explanation: Uses recursive traversal to process each nested object property, building dot-notation keys. This is useful for serialization, state management, or when interfacing with APIs expecting flat structures. The reverse function reconstructs the original nested shape, enabling round-trip conversions.

title: Format Duration to Human-Readable String
description: Converts a time duration in milliseconds to a human-readable string like '2h 30m 15s'.
explanation: The function breaks down milliseconds into the largest possible time units (months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds) and constructs a readable string. It avoids external dependencies by handling edge cases like zero or negative inputs and uses simple arithmetic with predefined unit thresholds. This is useful for logging, progress indicators, or any scenario where precise time representation needs to be user-friendly.

title: String Case Converters
description: Convert strings between camelCase, PascalCase, kebab-case, snake_case, and space case.
explanation: These functions normalize strings from any common format to the requested case. They handle edge cases like mixed input, multiple separators, and non-alphanumeric characters. Useful for consistent data formatting across APIs and UI components.

title: Truncate String with Ellipsis
description: Truncates a string to a specified length and appends an ellipsis if it exceeds the limit.
explanation: The utility provides two tightly related functions for truncating strings: one by characters and another by lines. It handles edge cases like word boundaries, custom ellipsis, and line length, making it useful for UI text rendering where space is constrained. The modern API supports optional parameters for flexibility without external dependencies.

title: Unflatten Nested Arrays by Path
description: Reconstructs a nested array structure from flat entries paired with their path indices.
explanation: This utility uses a Map-free approach with nested arrays to reconstruct a tree from flat entries with path strings. It handles sparse arrays and avoids mutating the input by building the structure from scratch.

title: Viewport Intersection Observer Utility
description: Creates a reusable IntersectionObserver that tracks whether DOM elements enter or exit the viewport with minimal reflows.
explanation: This utility abstracts the IntersectionObserver API into a reusable component that tracks which elements are visible in the viewport. It minimizes reflows by only triggering callbacks when state changes occur, making it efficient for lazy-loading, animations, or viewport-based triggers.

title: Circular Buffer with Fixed Capacity
description: A fixed-capacity buffer that overwrites oldest entries when full, providing O(1) enqueue/dequeue via circular indexing.
explanation: Uses circular indexing to achieve O(1) enqueue and dequeue operations without shifting an array. The fixed capacity makes it ideal for streaming data where memory usage must be bounded. Circular buffers are widely used in audio processing, network routers, and logging systems.

title: Memoize Function Results
description: Memoizes function results to avoid recomputation for identical inputs.
explanation: Uses a Map for O(1) cache lookups and stores results by serialized arguments. The default stringification handles primitives and simple objects/arrays, while the custom key function allows for more nuanced caching strategies like deep equality checks. This avoids recomputation overhead for pure functions with expensive operations.

title: Clamp a Number Within a Range
description: Ensures a number stays within specified minimum and maximum bounds.
explanation: The clamp function uses Math.min and Math.max to bound a value, ensuring it never exceeds the specified range. It’s useful for UI sliders, progress bars, or any input that must stay within valid thresholds. The wrap function rotates values cyclically, ideal for circular carousels or wrapping time around a 24-hour clock.

title: Retry with Exponential Backoff
description: Executes an async function multiple times with exponentially increasing delays between retries on failure.
explanation: The utility wraps an async function and retries it on failure using exponential backoff. It starts with a short delay and doubles it each retry until reaching a cap, preventing server overload. This pattern is useful for transient errors like network glitches or rate limits.

title: Strong Password Validator
description: Validates if a password meets common security requirements.
explanation: The isStrongPassword function uses regex patterns to check for required character types, providing flexible validation through an options object. isStrongPasswordWithEntropy adds entropy calculation to measure password unpredictability, helping avoid weak but complex passwords.

title: Deep Merge Objects
description: Recursively merges two or more objects without mutating the originals.
explanation: Uses Reflect.ownKeys to capture all enumerable keys including symbols, then builds a new object with descriptors to preserve property attributes. Recursion handles nested objects, while Object.create(null) prevents prototype pollution. This ensures safe, non-mutating merges even with complex object graphs.

title: Human-Readable Relative Time Formatter
description: Converts a date into a human-readable relative time string like 'just now', '5 minutes ago', or 'in 2 hours'.
explanation: The function calculates the time difference in seconds between the target date and now, then uses thresholds for each unit (seconds, minutes, hours, etc.) to return the most appropriate human-readable string. It handles both past and future dates gracefully and avoids complex dependencies by using basic arithmetic and conditional logic.

title: Mask Email Address
description: Masks part of an email address for privacy while preserving the domain.
explanation: The maskEmail function preserves privacy by showing only the first few characters of an email while masking the rest with asterisks. It handles edge cases like missing @ signs and ensures the masked portion is readable. The truncate function complements this by safely shortening strings for display purposes without breaking words.

title: Split Array at Given Index
description: Splits an array into two parts at a specified index.
explanation: The `splitAtIndex` function leverages native `Array.slice` for efficient partitioning, while `splitAtDelimiter` manually iterates to handle arbitrary delimiters. Both are useful for data transformation tasks such as splitting logs, parsing streams, or reorganizing datasets without external libraries.
